### Post by delb2k on 2007-02-04
Afternoon everyone,

Finally managed to install ubuntu 6.10 on my machine and its working very well apart from one issue. The resolution is stuck at 800x600. I`ve runa  command to get the xorg.conf and this is the result:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"

EndSection

EndSection

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R100 QD [Radeon 7200]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-40
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R100 QD [Radeon 7200]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"800x600" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"800x600" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"800x600" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"800x600" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"800x600" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"800x600" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection



Is there anyway that i can edit this file or change something so i can move it up to around 1240x700 (which is my current windows default). Please be aware that this is my very very first install, i`m still learning even the basics of command line OS systems, is there anything i can do to change my resolution at all or am i stuck with what i have?

Thanks everyone.

---

### Post by shareMenaPeace on 2007-02-04
Type 
```
gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
to edit - Backup before you do this it can lead to a system which want boot(Than you could use the command line or live cd to fix the file with).

Make sure to search the forum for radeon 7200 you miss a driver i guess.

---

### Post by irishhermit on 2007-02-04
*Is there anyway that i can edit this file or change something so i can move it up to around 1240x700 (which is my current windows default). Please be aware that this is my very very first install, i`m still learning even the basics of command line OS systems, is there anything i can do to change my resolution at all or am i stuck with what i have?*

Your file shows that the "highest" mode available is 88x600.  I had this problem with a Debian install.  This fix worked for me.  

While in Gnome I opened a terminal window, logged in as a superuser, and edited the file with gedit.  I edited the Screen Section as follows:  I removed the Depth and Modes that I didn't want, left the one that I did want, and created the new Mode that I needed.

Hope this helps.
Tom

My resulting edit:
Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R100 QD [Radeon 7200]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		**Modes		"1024x768"** 
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		**Modes		"1024x768"** 
	EndSubSection
EndSection

---
